text.title: Accessibility Automation QA Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Does the playbook step lack a named exit criterion?

conclusion.verdict: refuse the step until the exit criterion is named

branch.question: Is a decision branch missing an explicit signal?

conclusion.verdict: re-author with named tool output (axe rule id, severity)

branch.question: Did the team skip a WCAG check without logging?

conclusion.verdict: log the deviation; surface in next a11y retro

branch.question: Does the playbook lack a named owner?

conclusion.verdict: assign one owner who can authorize amendments

branch.question: Is the project pre-discovery or a one-off audit?

conclusion.verdict: use a one-shot a11y audit + recommendations doc instead
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
